title: Piliers Accomplis
sources: README.md: Phase 1 completion and mission overview
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District E
database.sql: Memo validation and user role status updates
forom-config.ts: Category E (Piliers) and Action 5 (Accompli) labels
App.tsx: Pixel reward and quest completion verification logic
resume: The quest 'Piliers Accomplis' (E5) represents the formal completion and validation of the community's thematic foundations within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of District E (Piliers) and the 'Accompli' action (5), this quest is the definitive milestone signaling that the core values and thematic anchors required for the node's decadal mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' have been established. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), reaching this coordinate means the community has successfully moved from initial ideation to a structured, archived reality. This is the moment where the 9 Super-Moderators confirm that the thematic DNA of the node is secure and ready for the 1209-user expansion. Technically, the completion of these pillars is archived in the local PostgreSQL database within the 'memos' table, ensuring that the structural heart of the project remains sovereign and private. The Forom architecture, built with React 19 and Vite 7, allows members to navigate these finalized pillars through a spatial 5x5 viewport, providing a clear visual representation of the node's stabilized identity. By hosting this data locally on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community ensures that its most precious cultural assets are protected from centralized corporate surveillance, adhering to the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy of total digital autonomy. Economically, completing the E5 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, supporting the redistribution of purchasing power as the server initiates its internal economy. This incentive structure is vital for recognizing the 'Creators' and 'Social' agents who have worked to build consensus and content around these pillars. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, E5 is the guarantee that the club's specific heritage is now safely canonized in the V1 Archive. Once the community reaches Phase 3: The Awakening, these finalized 'Piliers Accomplis' will be among the most important documents ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine. The AI will utilize this canonized data to ensure that all future interactions and developments remain true to the original mission. This achievement marks the transition from structural planning to cultural maturity, providing the necessary stability for the node to flourish in the upcoming global lobby. Ultimately, E5 transforms a collective vision into a permanent, actionable reality that secures the node's survival for the decade ahead. This entry is now canonized. Yes. Our memory is safe. Perfect. The foundations are complete. We are ready. Done. Mission secure.
summary: This quest marks the formal finalization and validation of the community's core thematic pillars at coordinate E5, securing the node's cultural foundations for the decade.
